The Anti-Launch Launch (Completed 05/08/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 1 of 4
Speaker 1: Hey guys, welcome back. We are gonna take a look right now at Theme Plate 9, which is
The Anti-Launch Launch. If you have access to the Six Figure Emails Theme Plate Pack,
then in Session Two, I believe, you're gonna have an even deeper dive into this one, a
full hour long session really detailing some extra nuances. But, if you don't have access
to that, this is what you're working with, don't worry, you have everything you need in
this mini lesson to really get this Theme Plate off the ground, into your autoresponder
and fired off on launch date to bring sales in right off the bat and launch in that
powerful way.
And what we're doing here with The Anti-Launch Launch is, this is the announcement,
this is when you're saying to the world, my cart is now open, my program is now
available, come and get it. But we're doing it in a way that's a pattern interrupt from the
typical launch hype that makes it a big deal. Instead, you want to get your prospect face
to face with the stakes and position the big deal, not about you and your launch and
how many years you've put into it and how your pets miss you and all this stuff, but just
the big deal is now how they can escape that pain. So keeping the focus on them and I
know it's hard, I know you put so much work into your launch, in not only building your
thing but also building the marketing and executing it, and it's so hard to take that focus
off us because we're proud of that and we should be proud of that. But there are other
outlets to experience it and express it and the launch date email isn't about you and all
this hype and craziness around what went into it.
It's about what your prospect can now overcome and escape, and just getting them
intimate with those stakes, show them that Rome is still burning. So here's the container
we're gonna use to do it. You're gonna lead with that link, your offer is now live, they've
seen enough in your pre-launch that they are ready to click through, then we're gonna
offer some social proof, or a core benefit of the product, we're gonna bridge into why
it's not an actual launch, and here's where it gets fun. So what is that Rome is burning
moment? What's burning? How have they been trying to extinguish it and describe that
existing problem and context as specifically as you can? This is where you dial up the
intensity of those flames, and then remind them that that fire was burning long before
you got there or it's been burning forever. So that's how you extend that consistency
principle into the indefinite past. Then you can get into why it's not a launch for you,
give credibility, proof, and case studies, all the things you've been doing for months or
years, that went into this.
So not just in the case of building this program for years, but the insights and lessons
and techniques have been, in the field, for years, and performing. So that's where you
extend your credibility into the indefinite past, if you will. Then you can call out a
negative trait, kind of like procrastination, and just hold them to that higher standard.
And then show them, in your call to action, the naturalness, the continuation of that
small step. Remember, it's a lot easier to escape that fire if it's a small step on flat land
than jumping out of a skyscraper, so make it as natural as possible. Remind them of the
cost of waiting, what will be left of Rome if you continue to stand here? Those flames
are just eating up more and more of it. Another call to action and then you can use the
p.s. to disarm one or two key objections and really try to get that sale. Right off the bat,
coach them right off the bat.

So, with all that said, here is an example of how we've done it, and this is a bit of a
longer email, but we're gonna enter it with courage and we're gonna escape this fire
together. So. 10X email is live but don't call it a launch. In case you missed it, we just
opened the doors to our 10X emails course, an awesome crowd of growth-minded
marketers, entrepreneurs, and copywriters have already punched it's one-way ticket to
email glory. The team plan has made that really affordable for bigger groups, you'll see
it here. Those fine folks are set to start generating revenue from the first few lessons
alone. So this is kind of a twist of that quick valuable win that you'll see more in Module
7, and the templates, we almost never offer templates around these parts, but we'd
rather teach you to fish than hand you a templated fish. We've got four email copy
templates for you in 10X emails. Four templated emails based on emails we've written
that have brought in 5 figures with a single send.
Four templates doesn't sound like a lot. Well, it's a drop in the bucket of awesomeness
that is 10X emails and it really only takes one solid email to cover your investment fast.
Challenge for you: try popping the upgraded FAQ email into your existing sequence and
not getting the sale, new sale notification pronto tonto, truly. And while 10X emails
might be the best thing we've ever shipped here at Copy Hackers, to call it a launch
would be nothing short of blasphemous. And now we're gonna position and gauge the
launch. So this is an extended intro that really kind of brought in that quickest and most
valuable win that you're gonna see in Module 7. So we brought that a little earlier, in
those intro call to actions, but now we're getting into the crux of it. Why isn't it a
launch? To you, to us, to the email marketing overlords who watch over your every
move, deciding if this is gonna be the day you finally have a major revenue
breakthrough. Hint, it could be.
Here, I like to get playful with our call to actions. It doesn't always have to be click here,
program now available. You could always weave it into the narrative and natural flow of
an email. 'Cause here's the thing, nothing has actually launched. Here's what I mean by
that and this is that Rome is burning moment for them. You've already been hard at it,
struggling to achieve just a sliver of those paydays, launches and user upgrades you
keep reading posts about. You already have a subscriber list or a clients list or a list of
trial users that's ready to be profited from. The struggle is transforming that list of
watchers into a list of customers. What aren't you telling them that they need to hear?
Why does it seem so easy for others and so damn hard for yours? Is there something
wrong with your lists or is something wrong with your offering?
So bringing back some of those moments of highest tension, those questions that really
kind of are present on their mind and you already own or work for an earlier growth
stage business that needs to maximize the ROI of every asset if it's gonna hit those lofty
benchmarks, everyone agreed on that Google hang out two weeks ago, back when it all
seemed so simple, back when you actually tried to generate revenue. So this is all that
super specific stuff and the more specific you are in describing that Rome is burning
moment, that's what makes the flames dance and that's what makes your prospect
more likely to jump out of it, it intensifies it, specificity intensifies those flames. See,
nothing has launched for you, you're knee deep in it and on our side, yes, it's true that

already doing and just make it better. And then we have our sign off and our p.s. we're
gonna address a key objection. Despite of the rumors, email marketing does not come
down to dumb luck, it's not one size fits all, it's about learning and applying a proven,
repeatable set of email, copywriting techniques. That's the difference between emails
that convert and emails that induce unsubscribed whip-lash.
So just kind of like a little joke there, not to be confused with keyboard face, where
you're dull, dull, dull email copy knocks your subscriber into a super slumber right at her
desk. Now, your emails don't have to be this long. This is more of a personality thing,
with Joanna and I, we do like to talk, we do like to write. So this could even be a little
self indulgent at times but you do have to have fun as a writer, especially if its
connecting and still entertaining your audience. Training program is also a total
misnomer so we could kind of overcome that second objection. No one actually wants
to invest time to learn copy for the sake of learning, they wanna arm themselves with a
skill to get a desired result and that's true no matter what kind of educational program
you're offering. The goal isn't to cram you full of concepts and jargon that make you
look 5% smarter at your next marketing conference. If you wanna look smart just name
drop Eugene Schwartz every chance you get. Done.
The goal here is much more direct. To arm you with the hot action techniques that you
can put in to work and profit from immediately. So this is something where using a lot of
repetition around that this isn't this crazy long training, it's about getting results right

So if you have an underperforming email rotting away in your drip sequence, a product
or service your subscribers need to believe in, someone that keeps hinting you're not
quite cut out for the job of generating revenue with email, totally worked for that guy
once, a client you really wanna wow the crap out of, a boss that would be far more likely

and applying it is a wasted moment. It's time to go with that natural flow instead of

and doesn't provide tingly feelings to CFOs.
So that is the Anti-Launch Launch Email. If you want the deeper dive, there is one
available for you in six figure emails otherwise don't be alarmed by the length, I know it
feels super long and intimidating. I've heard that before. Stick with the structure and see
how it plays out for you. Like all of these, it's not about length whatsoever. Length could
be very much dependent on you, you as a writer, you as a communicator. So if you are
just hitting these beats, coaching these important elements and it's 200 words, great. If
it 500 words, great. This one ended up being significantly longer but it's not about the
length, it's about the beats it's coaching, the actions and behaviors and beliefs and
thoughts and attitudes that it's coaching. So that's what I want to leave you with here.
This is Theme Plate 9 and the next one you're going to see a slight iteration on this one
that you'd be using if you're not pointing to a sales page and you need to give a little
more details on the offer itself right on the page itself. So I will catch you in the next
lesson for Theme Plate Number 10.
